result.summary: Found one actionable issue in the diff. I could not run the configured verification because `uv`, `pytest`, and `ruff` are not installed in this runner.

findings.title: Generated correlation token is never exposed to the client
findings.body: The new per-request token is created and logged server-side, but `/move` still returns only `move_uci` and the request schema does not accept any client-provided correlation ID. That means a browser/client cannot know this value and cannot use it for the stated client-side request correlation; operators will see opaque tokens in logs that cannot be matched back to client events. This is especially misleading because the helper names it a session token even though the Engine context says the server is stateless per request.
findings.recommendation: Either return the correlation ID in the response/header, or accept and log a client-supplied request ID such as `X-Request-ID`. If this is only internal tracing, rename it away from `session token` and make the log message reflect that.

next_steps: Verification attempted: `uv run --project src/engine pytest` and `uv run --project src/engine ruff check .` both failed because `uv` is unavailable.
Fallback checks also failed because the system Python lacks `pytest` and `ruff`.
